visa_types.name: Short-stay Schengen visa
visa_types.overview:
Schengen Rule 
The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination (determined by the purpose of the journey and the length of stay).
If your main destination cannot be ascertained, the Schengen state through which you first enter the territory of these countries is responsible for granting the visa.
Do I need a Visa for Germany?
Click here (http://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/EN/EinreiseUndAufenthalt/StaatenlisteVisumpflicht_node.html) to check if you require a visa to enter
                                                            Germany.
Schengen Visa and Visa Code
In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the
                                                            documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen
                                                            visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a decision on the visa application at its
                                                            own discretion, taking into account all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Manama to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 days. Applicants are therefore requested to organize themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment
                                                            system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more about the Visa application process, kindly click here.
Information on this website is subject to change, kindly check the information for updates before you apply.

visa_types.name: Culture Purpose
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
Additionally for Diplomatic and Special Passport holders
- Verbal Note from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Bahrain stating also the purpose of the visit.
Important information for Syrian Passport Holders
Passport Model issued in 2023:
The new Syrian electronic passports issued by the Syrian Arab Republic since 2023 are not in accordance with the ICAO requirements as there is no signature field. However, they will be temporarily recognized when they are signed by the bearer, except if the passport owner is younger than 10 years and in the case of minors for whom the issuing body has different regulations under national law. If the passport is not duly signed on page 2, the owner will have to sign the passport at a Syrian Embassy/Consulate.
This provisional recognition applies until July 31, 2024.
Passport Model issued from 2024:
The new passport model (ePassports) issued from 2024 will initially only be recognized on condition that the passport holder's signature has been provided on page 3 and their identity is sufficiently verified.
This provisional recognition applies until September 30, 2024.
Important information for travel documents for Palestinian refugees:
- Model 2023 of travel documents for Palestinian refugees is not recognized.
Checklist for Culture Purpose
Instructions for applicants below 18 years 
- For all children below 18years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX form. All Children also need to be present.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, the signed authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from Bahrain must be provided to complete
                                                                            the process.
- All applicants below 18 years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parent not travelling
Minors are required to complete the information about which school they are attending in the application form.
Furthermore, an official confirmation letter / school letter head is required to be submitted to confirm that the school granted permission to travel outside school holidays.
If this is not being submitted, it can cause delay with the visa application.
- Schengen Visa for House Employees (maid, nanny, driver, etc.) accompanying the sponsor
For Germany: From now on they will not be travelling under a visit or tourist visa but under a separate visa according to § 30 Nr. 2 with § 22 Nr. 9 BeschV which is for house employees.The type of visa will allow the applicant to accompany the employer to Germany for up to 90 days in 12 months.For the other countries represented by German embassy: They will still be issued a tourist / visit visa but with an additional remark saying that they have to be accompanied by their sponsor.Below documents are required in addition to the above-mentioned checklist
- Signed employment contract in Bahrain as house maid that includes salary, and date of employment
- From employer:
- Copy of passports
- Proof of residency
- Signed guarantee letter stating the following:
- All travel expenses incurred by the helper will be undertaken
- Travel details including address of chosen accommodation
- Reason why the helper has to travel along
- Bank statements of the last 3 months

visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for assessment of a Schengen Visa depends on the applicant's nationality. Applications should be made as early as possible (within 180 days ahead of arrival in the Schengen Area). Due to the higher number of applications during the annual high seasons (March to July, just before Ramadan or Eid al Adha) longer processing times are possible. Please Note: The processing time is an indication only. Applicants can always be requested by the respective Embassy to appear for an interview. The Embassy reserves the right to ask for further documentation.
The processing time for an application for a Schengen visa can be up to 15 calendar days in accordance with Article 23(1) of the Visa Code. According to paragraph 2, in individual cases up to 45 calendar days if the application has to undergo further checks. An exception can be made for cabin crew members and pilots. The passport will be returned by the Embassy via VFS only within up to three (working) days. If the application process has not been completed by then, the applicant must return the passport promptly via VFS only without being asked. Please understand that neither VFS nor the embassy have the capacity to inform you about the finalization of the visa in order to return the passport. Furthermore, please be aware that returning the passport beforehand may prolong the processing procedure.
Special Mention:
A visa applicant is free to request for an appointment directly at the embassy through writing an e-mail to
info@manama.diplo.de (mailto:info@manama.diplo.de)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Bahrain nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Embassy/Consulate of Germany in Bahrain.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
You may apply for a Schengen visa based on the country of destination, which means the country where you need to lodge your application, according to the Schengen rules.
These rules are as follows:
If you are going to visit only one Schengen country, file your application at the embassy/consulate/visa centre of that country
If you are going to visit more than two Schengen countries, file your application:
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where will be the main destination, or where you will spend most days if you will be spending an unequal amount of days in each
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where you will step first, if you will be spending an equal amount of days in each
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
Please Note:
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

- Signed employment contract in Bahrain as house maid that includes salary, and date of employment
- From employer:
- Copy of passports
- Proof of residency
- Signed guarantee letter stating the following:
- All travel expenses incurred by the helper will be undertaken
- Travel details including address of chosen accommodation
- Reason why the helper has to travel along
- Bank statements of the last 3 months

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are required to submit their Application form along a photo in the format, as explained in the pdf below:
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4,5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view sample photos
Requirements: 
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-24.5.5mm) of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

## Processing Time

Furthermore, please be aware that returning the passport beforehand may prolong the processing procedure.

info@manama.diplo.de (mailto:info@manama.diplo.de)

## Download Form
 Please click the below link for application form
Schengen/Short Term Visa Application Form - for Tourist, Business, Medical Treatment and Transit Category A 
- Now you can fill the form Online on Click Here (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt)
Once completed, print it and take the printout, including the two dimensional barcode on the last page, with you to the Joint Visa Application Centre. Instructions how to fill in your
                                                                        application you will find in the
General User Guideline to fill-in Schengen visa applications with VIDEX [pdf, 27.27k] 
To Download a Form: 
1. Click on the form link
2. When the form opens, save the form on your computer
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Bahrain nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Embassy/Consulate of Germany in Bahrain.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
You may apply for a Schengen visa based on the country of destination, which means the country where you need to lodge your application, according to the Schengen rules.
These rules are as follows:
If you are going to visit only one Schengen country, file your application at the embassy/consulate/visa centre of that country
If you are going to visit more than two Schengen countries, file your application:
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where will be the main destination, or where you will spend most days if you will be spending an unequal amount of days in each
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where you will step first, if you will be spending an equal amount of days in each
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
Please Note:
The visa fees will be charged 0 only when the applicants will be traveling with EU Spouse and case to case basis. 
In accordance with the Visa Code, visa fees are waived for the following persons:
- Spouses of EU-citizens
- Partners with whom the EU citizen has entered into a registered partnership on the basis of the legislation of a Member State, provided that the legislation of the host Member State treats the registered partnership as equivalent to marriage
- the relatives in the direct descending line of the EU citizen and the spouse or partner defined above who have not yet reached the age of 21 or who are dependent on them*
- the relatives in the direct ascending line of the EU citizen and the spouse or partner as defined above who are dependent on them*
*sufficient proof has to be submitted. 
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Bahrain nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Embassy/Consulate of Germany in Bahrain.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
You may apply for a Schengen visa based on the country of destination, which means the country where you need to lodge your application, according to the Schengen rules.
These rules are as follows:
If you are going to visit only one Schengen country, file your application at the embassy/consulate/visa centre of that country
If you are going to visit more than two Schengen countries, file your application:
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where will be the main destination, or where you will spend most days if you will be spending an unequal amount of days in each
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where you will step first, if you will be spending an equal amount of days in each
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
Please Note:
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Persons applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time
                                                                        is considered as a transit. The vast majority of travelers do not need a visa when air transiting through Germany. In Germany, the following airports have an International Transit
                                                                        Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:30 and 23:00), Düsseldorf (transit only possible between 6:00 and 21:00 and only if the airline has arranged the transit with airport
                                                                        security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:30 to 23:30), Munich (München).
Please check with your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal, you will have
                                                                        to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.
Please Note:
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Bahrain nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Embassy/Consulate of Germany in Bahrain.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
You may apply for a Schengen visa based on the country of destination, which means the country where you need to lodge your application, according to the Schengen rules.
These rules are as follows:
If you are going to visit only one Schengen country, file your application at the embassy/consulate/visa centre of that country
If you are going to visit more than two Schengen countries, file your application:
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where will be the main destination, or where you will spend most days if you will be spending an unequal amount of days in each
At the embassy/consulate/visa centre of the country where you will step first, if you will be spending an equal amount of days in each
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
Please Note:
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                                                            of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.
